messages.content: OK but what if I want my circle to Render an outline or a filled circle based on a color private members?
Ok but what is the relationship of ech of these SDL_RenderDraw methods and the Render(IDrawable drawable) we defined earlier, if we simple call these draw methods we don't need the Render method

I understood the concept but then I can call Draw methods directly instead of going to the shape, so the Render method in the shape can be avoided, I wanted to make my Renderer descentralized so the user don't go using the DrawPoint() directly instead of going Point.Render()

Which way do you think is better or more efficient? The friend method instead of the friend class sounds good to me but I don't have the expertise nor the knowledge for deciding which way is better for an engine like this, what do you think? Give me your opinions? I wanted to descentralize the Renderer but in the end if I don't do this friend method I won't be able to descentralize it, I wanted to do this in the first place because I have like 50+ methods of each of my shape, and that is not easily maintainable.

No i don't like that approach, The RenderContext just give away that I use SDL internally with is something I don't like, also all the methods of my Renderer will be moved there so bloating another class, I don't want this alternative, if there isn't anything else I may just move the SDL Rendering inside each Render method of each shape and call it a day, it is bad but having enterity in the Renderer is even worse. Also take into account I don't plan on changing SDL later on, I want the user to be 0 knowledge of how internally I manage it even though I use SDL; I don't plan on changing it so if that means sacrificing usability or complexity then I don't care about it being future proof for a probability of changing the SDL framework for something else in the future.
